meta.association: Biallelic pathogenic EIF2AK4 variants cause heritable PVOD/PCH and may also identify patients clinically classified as PAH who have PVOD/PCH biology.

evidence.reference_title: EIF2AK4 mutations cause pulmonary veno-occlusive disease, a recessive form of pulmonary hypertension.
evidence.snippet: recessive mutations in EIF2AK4 (also called GCN2) that cosegregated with PVOD in all 13 families studied

evidence.reference_title: EIF2AK4 mutations cause pulmonary veno-occlusive disease, a recessive form of pulmonary hypertension.
evidence.snippet: biallelic EIF2AK4 mutations in 5 of 20 histologically confirmed sporadic cases of PVOD

evidence.reference_title: Phenotypic Characterization of EIF2AK4 Mutation Carriers in a Large Cohort of Patients Diagnosed Clinically With Pulmonary Arterial Hypertension.
evidence.snippet: Genetic testing can identify these misclassified patients, allowing appropriate management and early referral for lung transplantation.

evidence.reference_title: Functional validation of EIF2AK4 (GCN2) missense variants associated with pulmonary arterial hypertension.
evidence.snippet: Biallelic mutations of EIF2AK4, which encodes the kinase GCN2, are causal in two ultra-rare subtypes of PAH, pulmonary veno-occlusive disease and pulmonary capillary haemangiomatosis.

evidence.reference_title: EIF2AK4 / pulmonary veno-occlusive disease and/or pulmonary capillary haemangiomatosis (Definitive)
evidence.snippet: EIF2AK4 | HGNC:19687 | pulmonary veno-occlusive disease and/or pulmonary capillary haemangiomatosis | MONDO:0018554 | AR | Definitive

nodes.description: Biallelic EIF2AK4 loss impairs GCN2-mediated integrated stress-response signaling and provides the established genetic mechanism for heritable PVOD/PCH.

biological_processes: cellular response to amino acid starvation

evidence.reference_title: EIF2AK4 mutations cause pulmonary veno-occlusive disease, a recessive form of pulmonary hypertension.
evidence.snippet: All mutations, either in a homozygous or compound-heterozygous state, disrupted the function of the gene.

evidence.reference_title: T-cell dysregulation and inflammatory process in Gcn2 (Eif2ak4(-/-))-deficient rats in basal and stress conditions.
evidence.snippet: The ISR was not activated after ASNase treatment in Gcn2-/- rat lungs, and apoptosis was increased.

evidence.reference_title: Pulmonary vascular remodeling patterns and expression of general control nonderepressible 2 (GCN2) in pulmonary veno-occlusive disease.
evidence.snippet: GCN2 expression was abolished in heritable PVOD (p < 0.0001), but also importantly decreased in sporadic PVOD (p = 0.03)
